note: xls_serialization registers `xls`/`xlsx` serialization formats (encoders built on phpoffice/phpspreadsheet), Views excel_export plugins, and a tiny global config form. All cases are `recipe` (response-graded), probing documented facts an agent would otherwise read source to answer; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: In PHP on a Drupal 11 site, I have `$data` (an array of associative rows) and want to turn it into an .xlsx spreadsheet string. Which core service do I call and what format string do I pass?
evals.expected_output: Use the core `serializer` service: \Drupal::service('serializer')->serialize($data, 'xlsx', $context) — the module registers `xls` and `xlsx` as serialization formats.

evals.prompt: What encoder classes and serialization format strings does this module provide for Excel? Name the classes and the formats.
evals.expected_output: Drupal\xls_serialization\Encoder\Xls (format `xls`, service xls_serialization.encoder.xls) and Drupal\xls_serialization\Encoder\Xlsx (format `xlsx`, a subclass), both tagged as `encoder`.

evals.prompt: This module errors because an external library is missing. Which Composer library does xls_serialization depend on to build Excel files, and how do I install it?
evals.expected_output: It requires phpoffice/phpspreadsheet (^2.4.0 || ^3.10.0), pulled in via Composer (drupal/xls_serialization requires it).

evals.prompt: I want a Views display that lets users download the results as a downloadable .xlsx file. Which display type / mechanism does this module use, and how is the format selected over REST?
evals.expected_output: A Views "Data export" / "Excel export" (excel_export) display, extending REST export, outputs the file; a REST resource returns Excel with ?_format=xlsx (or ?_format=xls).

evals.prompt: On a Views Excel export I want to output the legacy binary .xls (Excel 5) instead of .xlsx, and turn off HTML tag stripping. Which xls_settings keys control the writer format and tag stripping?
evals.expected_output: In the style's xls_settings, set xls_format to 'Xls' (Excel5 is remapped to Xls; default 'Xlsx') and strip_tags to FALSE (default TRUE); trim also defaults TRUE.

evals.prompt: Auto-sizing every column is slow on my large exports. Which config object and key disable column AutoSize globally, and which permission gates that admin form?
evals.expected_output: Set xls_serialization_autosize (truthy = disable AutoSize; default false) on the xls_serialization.configuration object at /admin/config/user-interface/xls_serialization; the 'administer xls serialization configuration' permission gates it.

evals.prompt: I want the header row of my Excel export bold with a colored background, and I want to embed the author and title in the file's document properties. Where are these configured?
evals.expected_output: Display options header_bold / header_italic / header_background_color style the header row; the style's xls_settings['metadata'] mapping sets document properties like creator, title, subject, keywords, company.

evals.prompt: My XLSX exports are huge and running out of memory with this module. Is there an alternative backend, and what is the trade-off?
evals.expected_output: Enable the xls_serialization_open_spout submodule, which decorates the xlsx encoder to use the faster, lower-memory OpenSpout library, at the cost of features like metadata, conditional styles, and auto-sizing.
